Title: The rice OsNAC6 transcription factor orchestrates multiple molecular mechanisms involving root structural adaptions and nicotianamine biosynthesis for drought tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator / non-protein amino acid derivative
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates iron, supports metal homeostasis, and may act as an antioxidant contributing to drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation promotes nicotianamine accumulation and contributes to drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported as specifically expressed in companion and pericycle cells of Fe-fed roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in roots of OsNAC6 overexpressors and down-regulated in nac6 roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; physiological drought assays using OsNAS activation-tagged lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; Fe status discussed from prior studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAS1 is a direct transcriptional target of OsNAC6 in rice roots. 2. Its expression increases in OsNAC6 overexpressors and decreases in nac6 mutants. 3. As a nicotianamine biosynthetic enzyme, it contributes to NA accumulation linked to drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering drought tolerance through increasing nicotianamine accumulation, potentially in combination with OsNAC6 or other NA-pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct functional transgenic drought evidence in this paper is strongest for OsNAS2/OsNAS3 activation-tagged lines rather than isolated OsNAS1 overexpression.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control gene for NA accumulation in this study context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator / non-protein amino acid derivative
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates iron, supports metal homeostasis, and may alleviate oxidative damage during drought
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression/activation increases nicotianamine and confers drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported as specifically expressed in companion and pericycle cells of Fe-fed roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Directly up-regulated in roots of OsNAC6 overexpressors and down-regulated in nac6 roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; drought assays of activation-tagged OsNAS2-D1 plants; chlorophyll fluorescence assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; Fe nutrition context discussed from prior work
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAS2 is a direct OsNAC6 target in roots. 2. Activation-tagged OsNAS2-D1 plants with high NA show enhanced drought tolerance. 3. This supports OsNAC6-mediated NA biosynthesis as a causal drought-tolerance mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering of drought tolerance through NA enhancement; can be stacked with root-specific OsNAC6 expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among NA genes, OsNAS2 has direct phenotypic support in this paper via activation-tagged lines.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator / non-protein amino acid derivative
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates iron, supports metal homeostasis, and contributes to drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of OsNAS3 increases nicotianamine and confers drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in roots of OsNAC6 overexpressors and down-regulated in nac6 roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; drought assays of activation-tagged OsNAS3-D1 plants; chlorophyll fluorescence assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAS3 is positively regulated in the OsNAC6 pathway based on transcriptome data. 2. OsNAS3-D1 plants with elevated NA are drought tolerant. 3. Elevated NA is therefore sufficient to improve drought tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to elevate NA and improve drought tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike OsNAS1/OsNAS2, direct OsNAC6 binding to OsNAS3 was not reported here; regulation is inferred from RNA-seq.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Methionine cycle linked to nicotianamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Methionine salvage cycle supplying S-adenosyl-methionine precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in the methionine cycle to help generate S-adenosyl-methionine precursor supply for nicotianamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator / non-protein amino acid derivative
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Fe chelation/homeostasis and drought tolerance via NA production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct OsNAC6 target that likely promotes precursor supply for NA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Directly up-regulated in roots of OsNAC6 overexpressors and down-regulated in nac6 roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDEP is a direct OsNAC6 target. 2. It is part of the methionine cycle supporting NA biosynthesis. 3. Its up-regulation is proposed to increase precursor supply for drought-protective NA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible stacking target with NAS genes to reinforce precursor supply for NA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Causal single-gene phenotypic validation was not performed in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly in root endodermis, pericycle, and phloem; also drought-, ABA-, salinity-, and low-temperature-inducible
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in roots of RCc3::OsNAC6 lines and in leaves plus roots of GOS2::OsNAC6 lines; native expression in root endodermis, pericycle, phloem
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator / non-protein amino acid derivative
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fe chelation/homeostasis and potential antioxidant function contributing to drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of nicotianamine biosynthesis and related drought-protective pathways
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsNAS1; OsNAS2; OsDEP; GSHT; SOT; GT; GAT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates direct target genes involved in nicotianamine biosynthesis, methionine cycle support, membrane modification, glutathione relocation, PAP accumulation, and glycosylation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Drought; abscisic acid; low temperature; salinity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Root-specific or constitutive overexpression enhances drought tolerance; root-specific overexpression improves field grain yield under drought; loss of function reduces drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major drought-responsive regulator that directly activates NA biosynthetic genes and coordinates multiple pathways underlying drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root-specific and constitutive overexpression; knockout mutant analysis; complementation; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; GUS; in situ hybridization; greenhouse and multiyear field drought assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Progressive drought; field intermittent drought; ABA; salinity; low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC6 is a drought-inducible NAC transcription factor necessary and sufficient for drought tolerance in rice. 2. It promotes root structural adaptations and directly activates genes including OsNAS1, OsNAS2, and OsDEP linked to nicotianamine biosynthesis. 3. Root-specific expression provides superior agronomic benefit under field drought versus constitutive expression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for drought-tolerant high-yield rice, preferably using root-specific expression to minimize reproductive trade-offs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive/strong whole-body expression can perturb reproductive development and reduce yield or cause vegetative growth retardation; root-specific expression performs better
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper centers on drought tolerance, but the nutrient-metabolism-relevant output is OsNAC6 control of nicotianamine biosynthesis and Fe homeostasis-linked protection.
